calibration_guidance: Use the full numeric range. High scientific upside is welcome only when the transfer is concrete, adapted, compatible, and calculable. It must also advance a consequential target-domain problem; mathematical sophistication, formal elegance, or interdisciplinary framing alone does not justify a high total. Apply a functional counterfactual: removing the connection should remove or materially weaken a solution, constraint, prediction, capability, or executable route; a change only in terminology, representation, or perceived elegance has no substantive value. A plausible analogy, an elegant reframing without substantive problem progress, or an incremental target application should not receive a high total.

marks.label: Intent Relevance
marks.guidance: Score alignment with the user's requested scientific outcome, target, and constraints.

marks.label: Transfer
marks.guidance: 15 requires a specific valid source ingredient, a nontrivial target adaptation, and a new target capability that concretely solves or advances a target-domain problem; loose analogies or decorative imports score near zero. Counterfactually removing the source connection must remove or materially weaken that capability or route. Mathematical sophistication or formal elegance without that problem progress also scores near zero.

marks.label: Target Contribution
marks.guidance: Score the importance and specificity of the new target-domain result as concrete progress on the target-domain problem, not the sophistication or elegance of its interdisciplinary reframing. The source domain need not receive a contribution. Substantial work begins at 14.

marks.label: Novelty
marks.guidance: Score publishable novelty of the combined source-to-target proposal if successful.

marks.label: Confidence
marks.guidance: Score evidence that the proposal is unclaimed after focused source-domain, target-domain, and intersection searches.

marks.label: Value
marks.guidance: Score scientific upside and whether target-domain experts should care about the completed result.

marks.label: Feasibility
marks.guidance: Score whether the bounded first calculation is physically compatible, executable, validated, and paired with a useful kill criterion.

marks.label: Well-definedness
marks.guidance: Score whether domains, translation map, setup, assumptions, observable, and deliverable are sharp enough to begin work.

total_score.label: Total
total_score.guidance: Set total_score to the closest integer after summing all marks. Eligibility is a separate hard gate and cannot be repaired by a high total.
